commands: backend: python manage.py test execution.tests_real_telegram_transport execution.tests_notification_transport
expected_results: The DEFAULT dispatch transport stays dry-run; the real transport is used ONLY when NOTIFICATION_DISPATCH_TRANSPORT is explicitly real/telegram-real/telegram.
Dispatch remains a no-op by default (NOTIFICATION_DISPATCH_ENABLED default OFF) — nothing sends unless dispatch is enabled AND the real transport is selected AND token+chat id are present.
The real transport fails closed (no network call) on a missing TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN or TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID; refuses non-WIN outcomes.
API ok=true marks SENT (transmitted=True); an HTTP/network error or ok=false marks FAILED (transmitted=False); the transport never raises.
Idempotent: an already-transmitted candidate is skipped (no re-send); the dispatcher's atomic claim + SENT terminal state prevent concurrent/repeat sends.
The bot token is never logged, returned in a DeliveryResult/detail, raised, or committed.
real_transport.py is the ONLY network-capable file in the notifications package; it references no order_send/order_check/ExecutionJob/create_contract/deliver_trade_result/wims.
No model/migration change; full backend suite green; governance + secret-scan pass.
actual_results: execution.tests_real_telegram_transport: 16 tests OK — selector defaults to dry-run; real only when explicitly selected (real/telegram-real/telegram; dryrun/empty/nonsense -> dry-run); missing token/chat id fail closed with urlopen NOT called; non-WIN refused (no network); API success -> ok/SENT/transmitted + urlopen called once with a Request to api.telegram.org carrying chat_id + text; HTTPError 401 -> FAILED (detail '401', no token); ok:false -> FAILED; idempotent skip when a prior transmitted delivery exists (no urlopen); dispatch disabled-by-default sends nothing (enabled False, 0 claimed, urlopen not called, 0 deliveries); enabled+real dispatch sends once + marks SENT + a second run does NOT re-send; dry-run dispatch makes no network call (transmitted False); token never appears in result.detail/repr/rendered_message or logs.

execution.tests_notification_transport BoundaryTests: the no-network guard now allows ONLY real_transport.py; real_transport IS network-capable (urllib.request) and no other notifications file is; real_transport references no order/WIMS surface.

Adversarial 4-lens review (default-off-gating / token-secrecy / idempotency-failure-marking / boundary-and-winonly, 5 agents) + opus synthesis: decision FIX with 1 HIGH must-fix. Default-off holds, token never leaks, WIN-only, fail-closed, no order/WIMS. Must-fix APPLIED: a duplicate-send window — the dispatcher recorded the transmitted=True delivery in the SAME atomic block as the SENT status update, so a finalize rollback erased the record, demoted the candidate to FAILED, and the belt (keyed off that row) re-sent. Fixed via _persist_transmission() writing the transmitted row in its OWN committed transaction before the finalize; on finalize failure a transmitted candidate is forced SENT (leaves the retry set). +2 tests (durable-across-finalize-failure; belt-skips-resend). 4 stale-docstring nits also fixed (dispatcher/__init__/dispatch_notifications/run_monitor_chain).
Full backend suite (post-fix): Ran 493 tests OK.
makemigrations --check execution: No changes detected. make governance-check OK; secret-scan OK; EVIDENCE-LINT PASS.
limitations: Repo-only. NOT deployed. NOTIFICATION_DISPATCH_ENABLED stays OFF, the default transport is dry-run, and no TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN/CHAT_ID are set in any tracked file — so NO real message is or can be sent by merging this. A real send requires a separate, Nuno-authorised arm step (store the token in the prod secret store + set the chat id + select the real transport + enable dispatch + deploy).

Idempotency: a real transmission is recorded durably in its own committed transaction (_persist_transmission) BEFORE the candidate can be demoted/re-claimed, and the transport's belt skips any already-transmitted candidate — so the finalize-rollback duplicate-send window the review found is CLOSED. The only residual is the irreducible dual-write case (the DB is unavailable for the single-row delivery insert immediately after a successful send); Telegram sendMessage has no idempotency key, so this narrow at-least-once tail is inherent and acceptable for the supervised demo.
The token is verified only by presence/length in the secret store (never printed); no getChat/API call is made with it in this packet.

TELEGRAM_CHAT_ID = @wims07072026 (the public channel username) is accepted directly by the Bot API — no numeric chat-id resolution or test send was performed (and none is performed without Nuno's explicit authorisation).
